name: Miami
localTips: Get an EASY Card for Metrorail and buses, but honestly a rental car or rideshare is how most visitors actually get around — Miami sprawls and the rail doesn't reach the beach.
Afternoon thunderstorms are a daily summer ritual (roughly June–September) — they usually blow through in 30–45 minutes, so plan indoor backups for 2–5pm and the sun often returns by evening.
Tipping is 18–20% at restaurants and bars; check the bill first, many South Beach restaurants auto-add 18% gratuity and double-tipping is a common tourist mistake.
Order a cortadito or colada at a walk-up ventanita window, not a sit-down café — it's cheaper, faster, and how Miami actually drinks its Cuban coffee.
South Beach parking and valet add up fast; use the SunPass-linked meters or a garage on a side street off Ocean Drive instead of valet where you can.

activities.title: South Beach Art Deco walk on Ocean Drive
activities.description: Pastel hotels with neon trim and porthole windows line Ocean Drive and Collins Avenue — the world's largest concentration of 1930s Art Deco, glowing best right after sunset.

activities.title: Wynwood mural streets
activities.description: Block after block of building-sized murals from Kenny Scharf to Shepard Fairey cover the warehouses around NW 2nd Avenue — free to wander any time, day or night.

activities.title: Calle Ocho & Domino Park
activities.description: Little Havana's main drag — cigar rollers in shopfront windows, salsa spilling from doorways, and Maximo Gomez Domino Park where elder Cuban men slap dominoes under a mural of Cuban heroes.

activities.title: Lincoln Road Mall
activities.description: A car-free, Morris Lapidus-designed pedestrian boulevard of palms, fountains and sidewalk cafés running eight blocks through South Beach — free to browse, buzzing till late.

activities.title: South Pointe Park at sunset
activities.description: The southern tip of Miami Beach — a spiral pier, cruise ships gliding past close enough to wave at, and the best free sunset-over-the-bay spot in South Beach.

activities.title: Bayfront Park & Bayside Marketplace
activities.description: A downtown waterfront park with the Klein sound-sculpture amphitheater, next to Bayside's covered, open-air marketplace of shops and bay-view bars — free to wander, rain or shine under its arcades.

activities.title: Miami Beach Botanical Garden
activities.description: A free, quiet two-acre oasis of palms, orchids and a lily pond tucked behind the Convention Center — locals' antidote to South Beach's noise, minutes from Lincoln Road.

activities.title: Coconut Grove waterfront & Peacock Park
activities.description: Miami's oldest neighborhood — banyan-shaded streets, sailboats bobbing at Dinner Key Marina, and Peacock Park's open lawn running right down to Biscayne Bay.

activities.title: Crandon Park Beach
activities.description: Key Biscayne's wide, uncrowded public beach with turquoise water and sea-grape shade — locals' pick over South Beach's crowds, with picnic pavilions right on the sand.
activities.priceHint: free entry, ~$8 vehicle toll to Key Biscayne

activities.title: Freedom Tower
activities.description: The 1925 Spanish-revival tower that processed thousands of Cuban exiles in the 1960s ('Ellis Island of the South') — its plaza and Mediterranean façade are free to admire on Biscayne Boulevard.

activities.title: Española Way pedestrian promenade
activities.description: A pink-and-orange, Mediterranean-Revival side street off Washington Avenue lined with cafés and art stalls — Al Capone once ran a card room upstairs at No. 1447.

activities.title: Miami Riverwalk
activities.description: A quieter waterfront path along the Miami River from Brickell to downtown, past yachts, tugboats and the historic Miami Circle archaeological site — few tourists find it.

activities.title: Books & Books, Coral Gables
activities.description: A Mediterranean-courtyard independent bookstore with a café and nightly author events — Miami's literary living room since 1982, and a genuine rainy-afternoon refuge.

activities.title: Colada at a Little Havana ventanita
activities.description: Order a colada — a shot of sweet, syrupy Cuban espresso split into tiny cups — standing at a walk-up window like Los Pinareños Fruteria on Calle Ocho, where locals actually drink it.
activities.insiderNote: A colada is meant to be shared — it comes with small cups so you pass it around; a single cortadito for yourself is the one-person order. Under $3, cash is easiest.

activities.title: Live salsa at Ball & Chain
activities.description: A restored 1935 Calle Ocho club where Billie Holiday and Count Basie once played — now a tropical courtyard bar with a live salsa band most nights and dance floor packed by 10pm.
activities.insiderNote: Arrive by 9pm on weekends to get a courtyard table before the band starts; free mambo and salsa lessons run some weeknights — check the calendar before you go.

activities.title: Cuban sandwich at Sanguich
activities.description: A tiny Little Havana counter turning out what many locals now call the best Cuban sandwich in Miami — pressed, dense with ham and pork, sold from a converted 1960s gas station.
activities.insiderNote: Order it with a side of the tostones and get there before 1pm — they routinely sell out of bread and close early on weekends.

activities.title: Candlelit sets at Jazid
activities.description: A red-lit, two-storey Miami Beach townhouse turned jazz-and-soul lounge with live music every night since 1994 — no dress code, no velvet rope, just musicians who actually play.
activities.insiderNote: No cover most weeknights; the upstairs balcony seats fill first, so arrive by 9:30pm for a spot overlooking the band. Cash-friendly bar tabs move faster.

activities.title: 1-800-Lucky hidden food hall
activities.description: Walk through an unmarked Chinese convenience store in Wynwood and out the back into a sprawling Asian food hall — ramen, dumplings, karaoke rooms and a full bar behind the fridge doors.
activities.insiderNote: The entrance really is through the working convenience store's cooler door — look for the neon sign around the corner if you get turned around. Karaoke rooms need a same-day booking.

activities.title: Cuban son at Hoy Como Ayer
activities.description: A tiny, unpretentious Little Havana club (the name means 'today, like yesterday') where exiled Cuban musicians have played son and trova since the 1990s — Miami's living-room jazz club.
activities.insiderNote: Wednesday's 'Fuacata' timba night packs the small dance floor fastest — buy tickets online, the room holds under 100 people and sells out.

activities.title: Morning swim at the Venetian Pool
activities.description: A 1924 coral-rock quarry turned into a Venetian-fantasy public pool with waterfalls, caves and loggias — 800,000 gallons refreshed daily, one of the most beautiful pools in America.
activities.insiderNote: Go right at the 10 or 11am weekday opening — it's a working neighborhood pool that fills up fast on weekends, and mornings mean cooler water and empty caves.

activities.title: Deering Estate at sunrise
activities.description: A 1920s stone mansion on a pristine slice of Biscayne Bay coastline south of the Grove — pine rockland, mangroves and an ancient Tequesta burial site, with a fraction of Vizcaya's crowds.
activities.insiderNote: Early-morning kayak tours through the tidal creek see manatees more often than the midday ones — book the sunrise slot if it's offered.

activities.title: Coral Castle, Homestead
activities.description: A jilted Latvian immigrant single-handedly carved and moved 1,100 tons of coral rock into a monument to his lost love over 28 years — nobody has ever fully explained how he did it alone.
activities.insiderNote: It's a 45-minute drive south in Homestead, well off the usual Miami loop — combine it with the Everglades entrance nearby to make the drive worth it.

activities.title: Kayak or paddleboard to Stiltsville
activities.description: Seven weathered wooden houses standing on pilings in open Biscayne Bay, all that remains of a Prohibition-era gambling and fishing shack colony — paddle out from Crandon Park for a close look.
activities.insiderNote: Rent a kayak or paddleboard from the Crandon Park marina and go on a calm morning — the crossing to Stiltsville is exposed to wind and boat wake by afternoon.

activities.title: Vizcaya Museum & Gardens
activities.description: A 1916 Italian Renaissance villa on Biscayne Bay, built for industrialist James Deering — stone barge breakwater, formal gardens and rooms crammed with centuries of European antiques.
activities.bookingQuery: Vizcaya Museum and Gardens entry ticket Miami

activities.title: Pérez Art Museum Miami (PAMM)
activities.description: A Herzog & de Meuron building draped in hanging gardens on the bay, showing international contemporary and Caribbean-diaspora art — the porch alone is worth the ticket at sunset.
activities.bookingQuery: Perez Art Museum Miami PAMM ticket

activities.title: Frost Museum of Science
activities.description: A three-storey Gulf Stream aquarium you descend through via spiral ramp, plus a planetarium and hands-on labs — a genuine rainy-day fix right next to PAMM on the bay.
activities.bookingQuery: Frost Museum of Science Miami ticket

activities.title: Wynwood Walls gallery compound
activities.description: The curated, ticketed courtyard where the Wynwood mural movement began in 2009 — rotating large-scale works by internationally known street artists in one walled compound.

activities.title: Everglades airboat tour
activities.description: Skim through sawgrass marsh at speed with alligators basking yards away — a day trip 45 minutes west into the 'River of Grass', Florida's defining wilderness.

activities.title: Jungle Island
activities.description: A tropical zoological park on its own island in Biscayne Bay — lemurs, orangutans and a walk-through aviary, an easy half-day between downtown and South Beach.

activities.title: Biscayne Bay sunset cruise
activities.description: A boat tour past Millionaire's Row, Star Island's celebrity mansions and the downtown skyline lighting up at dusk — narrated, with drinks aboard.
activities.bookingQuery: Biscayne Bay sunset boat cruise Miami

activities.title: Miami Heat game at Kaseya Center
activities.description: NBA basketball on the downtown waterfront — pregame bayfront tailgating and a crowd that treats every game like an event, in the arena that hosted three championship runs.
activities.bookingQuery: Miami Heat game tickets Kaseya Center

activities.title: Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden
activities.description: Eighty-three acres of rare palms, a butterfly conservatory and a rainforest walk in Coral Gables — an easy, shaded escape and one of the world's great tropical plant collections.
activities.bookingQuery: Fairchild Tropical Botanic Garden ticket Miami

activities.title: Bill Baggs Cape Florida lighthouse & bike trail
activities.description: Florida's oldest standing structure, an 1825 lighthouse you can climb, plus a flat, shaded bike trail through Key Biscayne's state park along an unspoiled Atlantic beach.
activities.bookingQuery: Bill Baggs Cape Florida State Park bike tour Key Biscayne

activities.title: South Beach food & history tour
activities.description: A guided walk through Art Deco backstreets stopping at Cuban, Latin and seafood spots most tourists miss on Ocean Drive — history and grazing combined.

activities.title: Jet ski rental on Biscayne Bay
activities.description: Rent a jet ski from a Haulover or Downtown marina and loop past Star Island's mansions, the downtown skyline and open bay — Miami's fastest way to see it all.
activities.bookingQuery: jet ski rental Biscayne Bay Miami
